title: Intense Smoky Egyptian Redcurrant Tagine
description: This tangy and smoky Egyptian dish features redcurrants and is perfect for those looking for a show-off meal with anti-inflammatory benefits.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Stir in the smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, turmeric, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper.
Add the redcurrants and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they start to release their juice.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove the cover and continue to simmer for an additional 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs or pomegranate seeds, if desired.
